records.ety: [AS. cuppe, LL. cuppa cup; cf. L. cupa tub, cask; cf. also Gr. ky`ph hut, Skr. kūpa pit, hollow, OSlav. kupa cup. Cf. Coop, Cupola, Cowl a water vessel, and Cob, Coif, Cop.]

defs: A small vessel, used commonly to drink from; as, a tin cup, a silver cup, a wine cup; especially, in modern times, the pottery or porcelain vessel, commonly with a handle, used with a saucer in drinking tea, coffee, and the like.
The contents of such a vessel; a cupful.
quotes: Give me a cup of sack, boy.

defs: Repeated potations; social or excessive indulgence in intoxicating drinks; revelry.
quotes: Thence from cups to civil broils.

defs: That which is to be received or indured; that which is allotted to one; a portion.
quotes: O my Father, if it be possible, let this cup pass from me.

defs: Anything shaped like a cup; as, the cup of an acorn, or of a flower.
quotes: The cowslip's golden cup no more I see.

defs: A cupping glass or other vessel or instrument used to produce the vacuum in cupping.

records.morph: [imp. & p. p. Cupped (kpt); p. pr. & vb. n. Cupping.]

defs: To supply with cups of wine.
quotes: Cup us, till the world go round.

defs: To apply a cupping apparatus to; to subject to the operation of cupping. See Cupping.
To make concave or in the form of a cup; as, to cup the end of a screw.
